content:
# Exodus Chapter 40 -- The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 112-113; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 83-84 | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_040_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_040_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_040_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_040_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## YHWH Commands the Erection of the Tabernacle (vv. 1-8)

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**2** "On the day of the first month, on the first of the month, you shall raise up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.

**3** And you shall place there the ark of the testimony, and you shall screen over the ark with the veil.

**4** And you shall bring in the table and arrange its arrangement, and you shall bring in the lampstand and set up its lamps.

**5** And you shall set the altar of gold for incense before the ark of the testimony, and you shall set the screen of the entrance for the tabernacle.

**6** And you shall set the altar of the burnt offering before the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.

**7** And you shall set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar, and you shall put water there.

**8** And you shall set the courtyard all around, and you shall set the screen of the gate of the courtyard.

---

## Anointing and Consecration Commands (vv. 9-11)

**9** And you shall take the oil of the anointing and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it, and you shall consecrate it and all its vessels, and it shall be holy.

**10** And you shall anoint the altar of the burnt offering and all its vessels, and you shall consecrate the altar, and the altar shall be a holy of holies.

**11** And you shall anoint the basin and its stand, and you shall consecrate it.

---

## The Priestly Consecration (vv. 12-16)

**12** And you shall bring near Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and you shall wash them with water.

**13** And you shall clothe Aaron with the holy garments, and you shall anoint him and consecrate him, and he shall serve as priest to me.

**14** And his sons you shall bring near, and you shall clothe them with tunics.

**15** And you shall anoint them as you anointed their father, and they shall serve as priests to me. And it shall be -- their anointing shall be for them for an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations."

**16** And Moses did according to all that YHWH commanded him; so he did.

---

## Moses Erects the Tabernacle (vv. 17-19)

**17** And it was in the first month, in the second year, on the first of the month -- the tabernacle was raised up.

**18** And Moses raised up the tabernacle, and he set its bases and placed its frames and set its bars and raised up its pillars.

**19** And he spread the tent over the tabernacle, and he placed the covering of the tent upon it from above, as YHWH commanded Moses.

---

## The Ark and the Veil (vv. 20-21)

**20** And he took and placed the testimony into the ark, and he set the poles upon the ark, and he placed the mercy seat upon the ark from above.

**21** And he brought the ark into the tabernacle, and he set the veil of the screen, and he screened over the ark of the testimony, as YHWH commanded Moses.

---

## The Table and the Lampstand (vv. 22-25)

**22** And he placed the table in the tent of meeting, on the side of the tabernacle northward, outside the veil.

**23** And he arranged upon it an arrangement of bread before YHWH, as YHWH commanded Moses.

**24** And he placed the lampstand in the tent of meeting, opposite the table, on the side of the tabernacle southward.

**25** And he set up the lamps before YHWH, as YHWH commanded Moses.

---

## The Golden Altar and the Screen (vv. 26-28)

**26** And he placed the altar of gold in the tent of meeting before the veil.

**27** And he burned upon it incense of fragrances, as YHWH commanded Moses.

**28** And he set the screen of the entrance for the tabernacle.

---

## The Altar of Burnt Offering and the Basin (vv. 29-32)

**29** And the altar of the burnt offering he placed at the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting, and he offered up upon it the burnt offering and the grain offering, as YHWH commanded Moses.

**30** And he placed the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar, and he put water there for washing.

**31** And Moses and Aaron and his sons washed from it their hands and their feet.

**32** When they went into the tent of meeting and when they drew near to the altar, they would wash, as YHWH commanded Moses.

---

## The Courtyard Completed (v. 33)

**33** And he raised up the courtyard all around the tabernacle and the altar, and he set the screen of the gate of the courtyard. And Moses finished the work.

---

## The Glory of YHWH Fills the Tabernacle (vv. 34-38)

**34** And the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of YHWH filled the tabernacle.

**35** And Moses was not able to enter into the tent of meeting, for the cloud dwelt upon it, and the glory of YHWH filled the tabernacle.

**36** And when the cloud was taken up from upon the tabernacle, the Sons of Israel would set out in all their journeys.

**37** And if the cloud was not taken up, then they would not set out until the day of its being taken up.

**38** For the cloud of YHWH was upon the tabernacle by day, and fire would be in it by night, before the eyes of all the house of Israel, in all their journeys.

---

> **END OF EXODUS (Shemot).** Moses finished the work, and the glory of YHWH filled the tabernacle. The cloud and the fire -- YHWH dwelling in the midst of his people -- will guide the Sons of Israel through every stage of their wilderness journey. The story begun in bondage ends in glory. The book of Leviticus (Vayikra) opens with YHWH speaking from within the tabernacle he has now filled.
